page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this server is positioned in the lower tier for compute-heavy workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng fall into the bottom 10% of benchmarked systems, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read performance at 27.8 GB/sec for large blocks, though write bandwidth for large blocks is average at 25.1 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve average performance tiers.

In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates weak to poor performance. Static web serving throughput is limited to 2.27 GB/sec, and database operations score in the bottom 25%. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the system processes 102.97 tokens per second for text generation using a small 135M model, but drops to a poor 4.57 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

Given the shared CPU architecture and resource profile, this server offers a budget-friendly option for workloads with low CPU utilization. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose tasks, basic web serving, development environments, or small-scale background utilities that do not require sustained high-performance compute or local storage.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show that the shared CPU allocation limits compute performance, with stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench scores falling into the lower percentiles. However, the server maintains average performance in single-threaded compression and small-model text generation. This resource profile represents a cost-efficient option for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for development environments, lightweight web serving, and basic background tasks that do not require dedicated CPU resources or high-speed local storage.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for low-intensity, budget-conscious general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM, ideal for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for lightweight, non-intensive utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
KVM hypervisor deployment with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average performance in single-threaded compression and small-model text generation
Suited for development environments, basic web serving, and utility tasks
